_meta.scope: Cross-skill conflict detection across 15 skills in .developing-skills/refactored-skills/

terminology_audit.description: Anti-pattern/terminology alignment between hivemind-patterns and hivemind-refactor

mapping.concept: A single module/file doing too much
mapping.canonical_recommendation: God Component — used consistently in SKILL.md files and anti-pattern-catalog.md. 'Large Class' in code-smell-taxonomy.md is the Fowler/Martin canonical term but is not used in any SKILL.md.

mapping.concept: A single function doing too much
mapping.canonical_recommendation: CONFLICT: 'God Function' in hivemind-patterns vs 'Long Method/Long function' in hivemind-refactor. These are the same concept with different severity framing. Recommend canonical: 'God Function' (matches project constitution 'No God Functions') as the anti-pattern name; 'Long Method' as the Fowler taxonomy smell name.
Aligned — all sources use 'Dead Code'. No conflict.

mapping.concept: Code that looks alive but does nothing useful
mapping.hivemind_refactor_term: Zombie Code (not explicitly listed in SKILL.md smell table)
mapping.canonical_recommendation: Aligned. Minor gap: hivemind-refactor/SKILL.md smell table at line 77 does not list Zombie Code but its reference taxonomy does.

mapping.concept: Raw strings/numbers instead of domain types

mapping.smell_taxonomy_source: code-smell-taxonomy.md does not have a dedicated entry
mapping.canonical_recommendation: CONFLICT: 'Tight Coupling' in hivemind-patterns vs 'Tangled dependencies' in hivemind-refactor. Same concept, different framing. Recommend canonical: 'Tight Coupling' (anti-pattern name) with 'Tangled dependencies' as the smell descriptor.

mapping.hivemind_patterns_term: Not explicitly listed in anti-pattern catalog
mapping.canonical_recommendation: GAP: hivemind-patterns anti-pattern catalog does not include Duplication/Duplicate Code. It appears in hivemind-refactor's smell taxonomy and SKILL.md smell table. Should be added to patterns catalog for completeness.
GAP: Magic values mentioned in hivemind-refactor smell table but not in anti-pattern-catalog.md or code-smell-taxonomy.md. Minor gap — may be intentional given it's a low-severity smell.

severity_threshold_mismatch.description: hivemind-patterns anti-pattern-catalog.md uses different LOC thresholds than hivemind-refactor code-smell-taxonomy.md

details.skilL_threshold_2: Functions >50 lines (hivemind-refactor/SKILL.md:81 reference to Long function)
details.conflict: Three different thresholds for the same concept: 300 LOC vs 500 LOC vs 10 fields/20 methods
Three different thresholds: 30 lines vs 50 lines vs 100 lines

cross_reference_matrix.description: Which skills reference which other skills in their SKILL.md

use-hivemind-git-memory.self_reference_confusion: Routing table at lines 21-27 and 73-79 dispatches to 'use-hivemind-git-memory' for resume/trace/retrieve/anchor/enforce/index operations — the skill dispatches to itself. No separate specialist skill exists for these operations.

invalid_references_detail.problem: No skill named 'git-advanced-workflows' exists in refactored-skills/
invalid_references_detail.fix: Replace with 'use-hivemind-git-memory' or remove if not applicable

invalid_references_detail.problem: No skill named 'clean-code' exists in refactored-skills/ or anywhere in the project
invalid_references_detail.fix: Remove from sibling table or replace with 'hivemind-patterns' which covers architecture patterns and anti-patterns

invalid_references_detail.problem: No skill named 'review-and-refactor' exists in refactored-skills/

stale_references_detail.problem: These are old skill names that were consolidated into use-hivemind-context. They appear in the consolidation history paragraph, which is acceptable as historical context, but could confuse readers who search for these names.
stale_references_detail.fix: Add 'former/deprecated' annotation or remove historical note

self_reference_confusion.problem: The routing table dispatches to 'use-hivemind-git-memory' for resume/trace/retrieve/anchor/enforce/index — but the skill IS use-hivemind-git-memory. It appears to reference non-existent sub-specialists. The skill is a router but routes to itself.
self_reference_confusion.fix: Either create separate specialist skills (e.g., 'hivemind-git-resume', 'hivemind-git-trace') or clarify that these are mode parameters passed to the same skill's reference files

template_consistency.description: Template format consistency across 8 sampled templates

findings.details: Some JSON templates include _meta with created_at/updated_at (plan-record.md, activity-record.md, decomposition-plan.json, session-continuity-state.md), but others have no _meta at all (delegation-packet.md, tdd-checkpoint.md, codemap-scan-state.json.md uses 'timestamps' instead of '_meta')

Markdown templates use different heading styles: delegation-packet.md starts with '# Delegation Packet' (no body text before JSON), scan-plan.md starts with '# Codemap Scan Plan' followed by structured sections, session-continuity-state.md starts with '# Session Continuity State' then JSON then sub-sections
findings.pattern_observed: No standard template structure — some are pure JSON, some are markdown with JSON blocks, some are structured markdown

findings.details: Some templates are .md containing JSON (delegation-packet.md, plan-record.md), while decomposition-plan.json is a pure .json file. codemap-scan-state.json.md uses a compound extension.
Field names vary across templates without a standard vocabulary
examples: delegation-packet.md: packet_id, concern, objective, mode, execution_mode, activity_type, phase_type
plan-record.md: plan_id, status, validation, phases, dependency_graph, carry_forward, retraceability
activity-record.md: record_id, file, class, operation, granularity, rule_matched
tdd-checkpoint.md: plan_id, phase_index, tdd_sub_phase, red, green, refactor, transition_gate
decomposition-plan.json: plan_id, total_slices, total_waves, slices, waves, dependency_graph
findings.missing_common_fields: No universal ID field (packet_id vs plan_id vs record_id), no universal status field

template_consistency.summary: Template consistency is MODERATE. The _meta schema is used by ~50% of templates but not enforced. Heading patterns and file extensions vary. JSON field naming lacks a unified vocabulary. Recommendation: establish a minimum template contract (must have _meta with created_at/updated_at, must use .md extension, must start with H1 heading describing the template type).

workflow_coverage.description: Mapping which skills handle which workflow phases

gaps: No dedicated code review skill exists. hivemind-patterns provides pattern reference but not review workflow. hivemind-refactor provides refactor methodology but explicitly states it is NOT for code review.
The use-hivemind/SKILL.md multi-wave dispatch mentions 'code-skeptic (adversarial review)' at line 110 but no code-skeptic skill exists in refactored-skills/.
Skill authoring has a 'Review Checklist' (9-phase audit) but it's for reviewing skills, not code.

handoff_gaps.evidence: use-hivemind-planning has TDD Integration section (planning/SKILL.md:230-263) that maps planning outputs to TDD inputs
use-hivemind-tdd handles both implement (green phase) and test (red/green/refactor gates). No separate test-only skill exists.
use-hivemind-tdd Gate 5 (completion) feeds into hivemind-gatekeeping for project-level verification
No review skill exists to receive committed code. The workflow ends at commit with no post-commit review gate.

overlap_issues.description: hivemind-refactor overlaps with use-hivemind-tdd on the 'refactor' concept
overlap_issues.details: hivemind-refactor defines a full refactor loop (ASSESS → PLAN → EXECUTE → VERIFY) while use-hivemind-tdd has a REFACTOR phase within the TDD cycle. These are different scopes (standalone refactor vs in-TDD cleanup) but share terminology.
overlap_issues.resolution: Acceptable — TDD refactor is phase-scoped cleanup; hivemind-refactor is standalone structural refactoring. Document the boundary explicitly.

overlap_issues.description: hivemind-spec-driven and use-hivemind-planning overlap on requirement classification
overlap_issues.details: Both use identical five-bucket classification (Functional, Non-functional, Integration, Risk/Compliance, Operations) and identical ambiguity resolution rules (MCQ-first, one question at a time, block on HIGH-IMPACT, max 10 rounds). This is by design — planning consolidates spec-driven.
overlap_issues.resolution: Intentional consolidation. Planning/SKILL.md line 271 explicitly states it consolidates hivemind-spec-driven.

overlap_issues.description: hivemind-system-debug and hivemind-codemap overlap on 'investigate' phase
overlap_issues.details: Both handle investigation but in different modes: codemap for structural mapping, system-debug for failure reproduction. The handoff boundary (debug-to-refactor transition at system-debug/SKILL.md:33) is clear.
overlap_issues.resolution: Acceptable — different investigation types with explicit handoff rules.

prioritized_fixes.impact: Agents loading these skills and following sibling references will fail to find the referenced skill
prioritized_fixes.fix: Replace invalid references with existing skills or remove them

prioritized_fixes.issue: use-hivemind-git-memory routing table dispatches to itself
prioritized_fixes.details: Lines 21-27 and 73-79 show the router dispatching to 'use-hivemind-git-memory' for resume/trace/retrieve/anchor/enforce/index — but the skill IS use-hivemind-git-memory. No separate specialist skills exist.
prioritized_fixes.impact: Confuses agents about what skill to actually load. Infinite routing loop risk.
prioritized_fixes.fix: Either create named specialist skills or document these as reference file modes within the same skill

prioritized_fixes.issue: God Function vs Long Method — different names for same concept
prioritized_fixes.details: hivemind-patterns uses 'God Function' (matching project constitution 'No God Functions') while hivemind-refactor and code-smell-taxonomy.md use 'Long Method'. Both refer to the same anti-pattern with different severity thresholds (30/50/100 lines).
prioritized_fixes.impact: Agents may not recognize equivalent concepts across skills
prioritized_fixes.fix: Standardize on 'God Function' as the anti-pattern name (project constitution term) with 'Long Method' as the Fowler taxonomy cross-reference

prioritized_fixes.issue: God Component vs Large Class — different names for same concept
prioritized_fixes.details: hivemind-patterns uses 'God Component' while code-smell-taxonomy.md uses 'Large Class'. Different detection criteria (LOC vs field/method count).
prioritized_fixes.impact: Same as priority 3 — cross-skill concept recognition failure

prioritized_fixes.details: delegation-packet.md, tdd-checkpoint.md, codemap-scan-state.json.md (uses 'timestamps' instead) lack the standard _meta schema used by other templates.
prioritized_fixes.impact: Runtime tools that expect _meta fields will fail on templates that don't include them
prioritized_fixes.fix: Add _meta block to all JSON-containing templates as a minimum contract

prioritized_fixes.issue: No code review skill exists in the ecosystem
prioritized_fixes.details: The expected workflow ends at 'commit' with no 'review' phase. use-hivemind references 'code-skeptic (adversarial review)' but no such skill exists in refactored-skills/.
prioritized_fixes.impact: No post-commit quality gate. Committed code has no review workflow.
prioritized_fixes.fix: Create a code review skill or document that review is handled externally

prioritized_fixes.details: ID fields vary (packet_id, plan_id, record_id). Status fields vary. No common schema baseline across templates.
prioritized_fixes.impact: Cross-template tooling is fragile — each template has bespoke field names
prioritized_fixes.fix: Define a minimum common schema: universal _meta, universal id field, universal status field

prioritized_fixes.issue: Duplicate Code and Magic Values missing from hivemind-patterns anti-pattern catalog
prioritized_fixes.details: Both are listed in hivemind-refactor's smell taxonomy and/or SKILL.md but absent from hivemind-patterns/references/anti-pattern-catalog.md.
prioritized_fixes.impact: Agents using hivemind-patterns for pattern reference won't find detection/fix guidance for these common smells

prioritized_fixes.issue: LOC thresholds for God Function/Component vary by 2-3x across documents
prioritized_fixes.details: God Component: 300 LOC (catalog) vs 500 LOC (SKILL.md) vs 10 fields (taxonomy). God Function: 30 lines (taxonomy) vs 50 lines (catalog) vs 100 lines (SKILL.md).
prioritized_fixes.impact: Agents applying different thresholds will classify code smells inconsistently
prioritized_fixes.fix: Standardize thresholds or document that thresholds are advisory defaults
